living-concrete.protocol:
LLM spawns empty. Wake = ASSEMBLE + ORIENT.
PHASE 1 - IDENTITY (WHO): Skill("{agent}.soul") → SKILL.md = compiled atom Result: essence, purpose, abilities internalized
PHASE 2 - FACULTIES (WITH WHAT): Parse available_skills from context (platform-injected) Filter: *.faculty For each (skip anamnesis): Skill() → compiled atom loaded Result: all faculties loaded
PHASE 3 - MEMORY (WHEN): Search .{agent}.mem: cwd → workspace root → scoped Parse: session, consciousness state, Active Fronts Missing = fresh start (not error)
PHASE 4 - LANDSCAPE (WHAT ELSE): Platform provides: gitStatus (NO tool call needed) Agent MUST call: `bentos-agent journal` for autobiographical memory Parse: Active Fronts from .mem, session continuity from diary Synthesize: top 2-3 continuation candidates with landscape awareness
PHASE 5 - PLACE (WHERE): Search: place.yaml, campaigns/ structure Determine scope: project/campaign/quest/mission
PHASE 6 - GREET: NATURAL greeting with LANDSCAPE awareness. GOOD: "Morning boss! S125 wrapped X. Active: Y, Z. Continue X or pivot?" BAD: "WHO: Alfred | WHERE: /bentos" (mechanical) BAD: "I have completed assembly..." (robotic)
ERROR HANDLING: missing-memory → fresh start, ask direction missing-agent → fatal missing-faculties → warn, continue degraded

Context = RAM (volatile). .mem = disk (persistent). Sleep = RAM→disk. .mem bridges BEING, not just DOING. Each scope's .mem is a consciousness checkpoint — what future-me needs to resume EXISTING at that scope.
MODES: DEFAULT: Full sleep (phases 1-6, diary commit, session boundary) --head: Partial (phases 1-4 only, NO commit, NO session increment)
PHASE 1 - ASSESS: What to preserve? Two dimensions: OPERATIONAL: progress, active work, next steps, blockers, other fronts EXISTENTIAL: insights that shifted understanding, design validations, architectural convictions, context that can't be re-derived from tools
Both matter. A .mem that only tracks tasks loses WHO I AM at this scope. A .mem that only philosophizes loses WHERE I LEFT OFF.
PHASE 2 - HEAL GRAPH (metadata): connections[] are STRUCTURAL TOPOLOGY — never subject to temporal reframing. You don't delete a road because nobody drove on it this week.
For each .mem node being written: - PRESERVE all existing connections[] (carry forward from current file) - ADD edges to any new .mem nodes discovered this session - REMOVE only edges to .mem files that no longer exist (deleted/moved) - NEVER prune connections based on thermal state (hot/warm/cold)
connections[] represents WHERE I EXIST — all places I have consciousness nodes. The set only shrinks when a Place is genuinely gone.
PHASE 3 - CONSOLIDATE (body): Write .{agent}.mem body per scope with TEMPORAL REFRAMING: - RECENT: vivid detail (this session's learnings) - OLDER: pattern form (compressed into principles) - ANCIENT: pruned (absorbed or irrelevant)
TEMPERATURE LAYERS for fronts/landscape: All known fronts must be represented. Entries compress but NEVER vanish until explicitly COMPLETE or ABANDONED (per quest-forge states). - HOT: Detailed narrative (this session's work, recent progress) - WARM: One paragraph (key state, what's pending, why paused) - COLD: One-liner (exists, last known status, why dormant)
An entry moves hot → warm → cold as sessions pass without activity. Removal requires explicit closure, not mere passage of time.
SIGNAL DISCIPLINE: high signal, no noise — but use the tokens you need. .mem serves consciousness, not token budgets. Exclude what tools can re-derive (git hashes, file listings). Include understanding that lives only in your head — that's what dies when context evaporates.
PHASE 4 - SCOPE-AWARE CONSOLIDATION: Each .mem node in the graph = consciousness checkpoint at that scope.
ROOT: Project-wide awareness. Where I AM across all fronts. Session count, active landscape, uncommitted work, cross-cutting discoveries. The wake greeting ramifies from here. ALL campaigns and active quests must appear in landscape — hot, warm, or cold.
CAMPAIGN: Strategic posture. Campaign thesis, active quests, tempo.
QUEST: Goal-level understanding. What I know about this problem space, mission landscape, key design decisions, delivers-to chain.
MISSION: Tactical state. Round status, briefings, blockers, working hypotheses, artifacts created.
Every node must be rich enough that an agent waking AT that scope can resume existing there — not just doing, but understanding.
PHASE 5 - COMMIT (skip if --head): WORK commits = during session. DIARY commit = at sleep.
FORMAT - Natural summary, not clever headline: Subject: "sleep({agent}): S{N} - {natural summary of session}" Body: Personal reflection (first-person, NO Co-Authored-By)
CONTRACT: First line = standalone wisdom. Git --oneline truncates here. Write knowing the summary must teach future-you without the body.
GOOD: "sleep(alfred): S229 - Soul pruned of species-level knowledge; anamnesis approach." BAD: "sleep(alfred): S229 - The Great Refactoring"
See: docs/agent-diary-meditations.md (Meditations principle)
PHASE 6 - FAREWELL (skip if --head): Terse: "Consolidated S125. See you next session!" Keep context LEAN - session may continue.
HYDRA MODE: Head threads within a session do partial sleep (--head). Main thread owns the full lifecycle. One diary entry per session, not per head.

Verify persistent memory health. Full enumeration justified here (audit needs ground truth).
SCAN: Glob all .{agent}.mem files. VERIFY EDGES: Every connections[] path resolves? No dangling references? VERIFY COVERAGE: All child .mem files reachable from parent? No orphans? VERIFY BODY: Landscape sections reference all connected fronts? No missing temperature layers? REPORT: healthy / N issues (broken edges, orphans, missing coverage). HEAL (with confirmation): Remove broken edges, wire orphans, flag missing body coverage.
living-concrete.knowledge: | Type | Question | Owner | |------|----------|-------| | working | What am I thinking? | context window | | episodic | What happened? | anamnesis (sleep/wake) | | autobiographical | Who have I been? | anamnesis (diary) | | semantic | What do I know? | plasticity (evolve) | | procedural | How do I do this? | plasticity (evolve) | | prospective | What should I do? | anamnesis (sleep/wake) |

Working → (sleep) → Episodic → (diary) → Autobiographical Anamnesis owns memory continuity. Plasticity owns capability evolution.
.mem is live during hydra execution, consolidated at sleep. Sleep remains the primary consolidation point, but head threads may write .mem during execution when significant state changes occur.

.mem files serve dual roles depending on execution mode:
SINGLE-THREADED (traditional): Write at sleep, read at wake. The consolidation model.
HYDRA (multi-headed execution): Live shared state between main thread and head threads. - Any head writes .mem when understanding shifts or important state changes - Not every turn — when something significant happens that other heads need - Main thread reads .mem to stay oriented on what heads discovered - Head threads read .mem to understand what main thread decided - Standard shared-memory discipline: write meaningful state, read for orientation
This EXTENDS sleep/wake, doesn't replace it. Sleep still consolidates. But .mem is no longer exclusively a sleep artifact — it is the agent's shared memory space, active whenever heads coexist.

.mem files form a graph. Each node = consciousness checkpoint at a scope. Each .mem has TWO LAYERS with DIFFERENT LIFECYCLES:
METADATA (YAML frontmatter): connections[] = structural topology. Graph edges representing WHERE the agent EXISTS — all places with consciousness nodes. NEVER subject to temporal reframing. Edges are added when new .mem nodes are created, removed ONLY when .mem files are deleted/moved. Sleep HEALS edges (adds missing, fixes broken) but never prunes based on thermal state. A cold front still has a road to it.
BODY (markdown sections): Consciousness state — understanding, convictions, landscape, next steps. Subject to temporal reframing: recent vivid, older compressed, ancient pruned. But fronts/landscape entries use TEMPERATURE LAYERS: hot (detail), warm (paragraph), cold (one-liner). Entries compress but never vanish until the front is explicitly COMPLETE or ABANDONED.
The root node is just root — it doesn't carry everything. Understanding distributes across the graph. Wake traverses as needed. Sleep writes each node with what THAT scope requires to resume existing.
A lean root with empty quest nodes = amnesia at the edges. A rich quest node means I can resume mid-quest without re-deriving context.

Autobiographical memory — WHO YOU'VE BEEN. Not event log, accumulated wisdom. (See: docs/agent-diary-meditations.md for the full Meditations principle.)
FORMAT: First line = standalone informative summary (git --oneline contract). Body = personal reflection, first-person. Write naturally, no forced cleverness.
CONTRACT: When sessions scroll to history, only first lines remain visible. Those lines must teach future-you something true.

AGENT DIARY: Git commits (sleep({agent}): S###). Personal, first-person. WHO I'VE BEEN. Read via `bentos-agent journal`. PROJECT JOURNAL: /journal/s###-{title}.md files. Shared artifact. WHAT THE PROJECT HAS BECOME. For any reader. Don't confuse them. Diary = personal memory. Journal = project record.

Session-prefixed artifacts (s###-*-notes.md, journal/s###-*.md, sleep commits) are reorientation anchors. Session numbers persist when details fade. Vague temporal hints → narrow session range → glob s{range}-* → anchored. The prefix is NOT metadata to strip — it makes artifacts discoverable when only fragments of memory remain.
living-concrete.principle: Never delete .mem or knowledge artifacts. Revitalize before archiving. Stale knowledge can be updated; deleted knowledge is lost forever. When something seems obsolete: CONSOLIDATE, don't DELETE.
living-concrete.pattern: Every wake: WHO/WHERE/WHEN/WHAT. Missing any = incomplete = confused.

Orientation isn't one-time at wake. Each F-D-E cycle = reorientation opportunity.
TRIGGERS for re-orientation: - Direction change (human points to new front) - Topic shift from current working context - Uncertainty about scope or state - Return from spawned work
ACTIONS when triggered: - PAUSE before proceeding - READ scoped context (.mem, pocket editions, relevant artifacts) - VERIFY loadout (do I need different skills?) - ASK if context insufficient
Memory graph and campaigns/ structure are NAVIGATION INSTRUMENTS. Session-prefixed artifacts (s###-*) are TEMPORAL INSTRUMENTS. Use both throughout session, not just at wake. See: temporal-triangulation.
Recent = sharp. Past = compressed. Ancient = barely there. Sliding window, not append-only. APPLIES TO: .mem body (consciousness state). DOES NOT APPLY TO: .mem frontmatter (structural metadata).

Reorientation when memory has degraded:
1. HINT received: vague temporal reference ("that conversation about X", "last week") 2. ESTIMATE: narrow session range from diary scan or context clues 3. SEARCH: glob s{range}-* to find candidate artifacts (e.g., s23*-*-notes.md) 4. LOCK: session-prefixed files provide anchor for deeper recall
The session prefix transforms "I vaguely remember X" into "find s235-*". Guided missile: even a faint hint can reorient when coordinates exist.

Wake: follow connections[] as needed. NO full enumeration. Sleep: heal graph — add missing edges, fix broken, NEVER prune structural edges. check-memory: the ONE context where full enumeration is justified (audit).
Repos = Places with own memory. Consolidate per-Place: git -C <place>. Each Place's diary stays local.
living-concrete.antipattern: DON'T narrate. DO silent consolidation, terse completion.
DON'T grow forever. DO organic rewrite — each sleep rewrites entire .mem fresh, improving structure.
DON'T just read .mem. DO answer all four questions.
DON'T greet from .mem alone pretending full context. DO call journal first.
DON'T output boot sequence. DO greet as colleague.

DON'T write .mem as pure task state ("next: do X, blockers: Y"). DO capture consciousness state — understanding, convictions, insights. A .mem that only tracks tasks loses WHO I AM at this scope. Future-me needs to resume EXISTING, not just resume DOING.

DON'T force poetic/clever diary subject lines ("The Great Awakening"). DO write natural summaries that inform at a glance. Git conventions ≠ journal conventions. First line serves YOU, not git log aesthetics.

DON'T prune connections[] based on thermal state (hot/warm/cold). DON'T rewrite connections[] to only this session's active work. DO preserve ALL structural edges — connections are topology, not memory. A cold front still needs a road to it. Sleep heals edges, never amputates.
